meanings.usage: 后接名词、代词或动名词作宾语，不可接不定式
常与介词on/upon搭配，表示对……的影响
固定搭配pride oneself on/upon sth./doing sth.
不可数名词，可与take pride in搭配
表示抽象品味时为不可数名词，表口味时可作可数名词
作系动词时后接形容词作表语
常与influence, pressure, effort等名词搭配
多用来形容变化、影响、差别等抽象事物
常用搭配persuade sb. to do sth. 或persuade sb. into doing sth.
常与介词into搭配，表示将……分为……
及物动词，宾语多为计划、方案、装置等
capture one's attention为固定搭配，表示吸引某人的注意
可用来形容数量、程度、反应等，非正式语境中也可表示“极好的”
可指占据空间、时间，也可表示占领、担任职位
语气比big, large强，强调规模异常大
可数名词，多指食品的成分，也可引申为要素、因素
后可接名词、动名词或that从句作宾语

phrases.usage: 表示免费获得某物、不劳而获，口语和书面语中都常用

examples.en: There is no such thing as getting something for nothing in the world.
examples.zh: 世界上没有不劳而获的好事。

examples.en: Many people are attracted by the idea of getting something for nothing.
examples.zh: 很多人都被免费得东西的想法吸引。

phrases.usage: 表示对……施加影响，可在influence前加形容词修饰影响的性质

examples.en: Parents exert a great influence on their children's character development.
examples.zh: 父母对孩子的性格发展有很大影响。

examples.en: Social media exerts a subtle influence on people's values.
examples.zh: 社交媒体对人们的价值观有着微妙的影响。

phrases.usage: 为……感到自豪、得意，主语通常是人

examples.en: He prides himself on his ability to speak four languages fluently.
examples.zh: 他为自己能流利说四种语言而自豪。

examples.en: The restaurant prides itself on using only fresh local ingredients.
examples.zh: 这家餐厅以只使用新鲜本地食材为荣。

phrases.usage: 使某人能够做某事，是正式表达，常用于说明某事物的作用

examples.en: The new policy enables low-income families to afford better housing.
examples.zh: 新政策让低收入家庭能够负担得起更好的住房。

examples.en: The Internet enables people to communicate with others around the world easily.
examples.zh: 互联网让人们可以轻松和世界各地的人交流。

phrases.usage: 吸引……的注意力，同义表达还有draw/attract one's attention

examples.en: The bright color of the poster captured the attention of passers-by.
examples.zh: 海报鲜艳的颜色吸引了路人的注意。

examples.en: His speech at the conference captured the attention of all the attendees.
examples.zh: 他在会议上的发言吸引了所有参会者的注意。

phrases.usage: 对……的反应、回应，response后接介词to

examples.en: The response to the new product launch was beyond our expectation.
examples.zh: 新产品发布后的反响超出了我们的预期。

examples.en: There has been a positive response to the government's new environmental policy.
examples.zh: 公众对政府新的环保政策反应积极。

phrases.usage: 不久之后，相当于soon，可放在句首或句末

examples.en: Before long, he got used to the new school life and made many friends.
examples.zh: 不久之后他就适应了新的学校生活，还交了很多朋友。

examples.en: The project is progressing well, and before long we will see the results.
examples.zh: 项目进展顺利，很快我们就能看到成果了。

phrases.usage: 各种形状和大小的，用来形容事物种类多样

examples.en: The store sells bags of all shapes and sizes to meet different customers' needs.
examples.zh: 这家店卖各种形状和尺寸的包，满足不同顾客的需求。

examples.en: On the beach, there are shells of all shapes and sizes scattered everywhere.
examples.zh: 沙滩上散落着各种形状和大小的贝壳。

grammar.title: as引导的让步状语从句
grammar.definition: as引导让步状语从句时，需要将表语、状语或动词原形提前到句首，形成倒装结构，含义相当于although/though
grammar.structure: 形容词/副词/名词/动词原形 + as + 主语 + 谓语，主句
grammar.usage: 用于正式语境中表达“尽管、虽然”的含义，若提前的是单数可数名词，前面要省略冠词

examples.en: Much as we may pride ourselves on our good taste, we are no longer free to choose the things we want.
examples.zh: 尽管我们可能对自己的品味很是得意，但我们已经不能自主选择想买的东西了。

examples.en: Young as he is, he has already won several international awards in the field of physics.
examples.zh: 尽管他很年轻，他已经在物理领域获得了数个国际奖项。

examples.en: Try as she might, she could not open the locked door without the key.
examples.zh: 无论她怎么努力，没有钥匙她都打不开那扇锁着的门。

grammar.title: 动名词的被动语态
grammar.definition: 当动名词的逻辑主语是动名词动作的承受者时，动名词需要用被动形式，即being + 过去分词
grammar.structure: 动词（后接动名词） + being done
grammar.usage: 常用于avoid, enjoy, mind, remember, forget等后接动名词作宾语的动词之后

examples.en: No one can avoid being influenced by advertisements.
examples.zh: 没有人能避免受到广告的影响。

examples.en: He was very upset about being criticized by the teacher in front of the whole class.
examples.zh: 他因为在全班面前被老师批评而非常难过。

examples.en: Many young people dream of being admitted to their ideal universities.
examples.zh: 很多年轻人都梦想被自己理想的大学录取。

grammar.title: make it + 形容词 + for sb. to do sth.
grammar.definition: 该结构中it是形式宾语，真正的宾语是后面的不定式短语，形容词作宾语补足语，用来补充说明做某事的性质
grammar.structure: make + it + 形容词/名词 + (for sb.) + 不定式短语
grammar.usage: 当不定式作宾语且后面有宾语补足语时，需要用it作形式宾语，将真正的宾语后置，保持句子结构平衡

examples.en: Radio and television have made it possible for advertisers to capture the attention of millions of people in this way.
examples.zh: 广播和电视使得广告商能够用这种方式吸引数百万人的注意。

examples.en: Smartphones make it convenient for people to shop, study and work anywhere.
examples.zh: 智能手机让人们可以随时随地很方便地购物、学习和工作。

examples.en: The frequent public transport makes it easy for citizens to travel around the city.
examples.zh: 便捷的公共交通让市民在城市里出行很容易。

sentencePatterns.pattern: Much as we may ..., we ... for ...

original.en: Much as we may pride ourselves on our good taste, we are no longer free to choose the things we want, for advertising exerts a subtle influence on us.
original.zh: 尽管我们可能对自己的品味很是得意，但我们已经不能自主选择想买的东西了，因为广告在我们身上施加着微妙的影响。

imitations.en: Much as we may value our independence, we often need help from others, for no one can solve all problems alone.
imitations.zh: 尽管我们可能很看重自己的独立性，但我们时常需要他人的帮助，因为没有人能独自解决所有问题。

imitations.en: Much as we may enjoy staying up late, we should form the habit of sleeping early, for regular rest is essential to our health.
imitations.zh: 尽管我们可能很享受熬夜，但我们应该养成早睡的习惯，因为规律的休息对我们的健康至关重要。

sentencePatterns.pattern: It was so + 形容词 + that ...

original.en: It was so heavy that a crane had to be used to remove it from the lorry.
original.zh: 它实在太重了，以至于得用起重机才能把它从卡车上卸下来。

imitations.en: The novel was so interesting that I stayed up the whole night to finish reading it.
imitations.zh: 这本小说太有趣了，以至于我熬了一整夜把它读完了。

imitations.en: The lecture was so boring that most of the audience fell asleep halfway through.
imitations.zh: 这场讲座太无聊了，以至于大多数听众听到一半都睡着了。

sentencePatterns.pattern: ... had to ... than they had anticipated, for ...

original.en: The manufacturers had to pay more money than they had anticipated, for they bought the biscuit from the student for $24,000.
original.zh: 生产商不得不付出比预期多得多的钱，因为他们花了24000美元从那个学生手里买下了这块饼干。

imitations.en: We had to spend more time on the project than we had anticipated, for there were many unexpected difficulties in the process.
imitations.zh: 我们在这个项目上花的时间比预期要多，因为过程中出现了很多意料之外的困难。

imitations.en: The travel agency had to arrange more accommodation than they had anticipated, for the number of tourists doubled during the holiday.
imitations.zh: 旅行社不得不安排比预期更多的住宿，因为假期期间游客数量翻了一倍。

original.en: Advertisers discovered years ago that all of us love to get something for nothing.
original.zh: 广告商多年前就发现，我们所有人都喜欢免费得到东西。

imitations.en: Educators discovered years ago that children learn better in a relaxed and interactive environment.
imitations.zh: 教育工作者多年前就发现，孩子在轻松互动的环境里学习效果更好。

imitations.en: Scientists discovered years ago that regular exercise can reduce the risk of many chronic diseases.
imitations.zh: 科学家多年前就发现，规律锻炼可以降低很多慢性病的患病风险。
